notes: seed / PR #1758 reference

hypothesis: Lower muon_momentum_warmup_start from 0.92 to 0.85, matching the canonical modded-nanogpt recipe; keeps final momentum 0.99 and 0.33 warmup duration intact. Mechanism: early high momentum (0.92) over-smooths the informative early-training gradient signal during the high-LR phase; starting at 0.85 lets the model adapt faster in the first ~10% of the budget while still saturating to 0.99 well before warmdown.
notes: Single-knob meta tweak. Muon momentum start was unusually high vs canonical 0.85; AdamW knobs reported crowded so picked a Muon axis instead. Risk: if 0.92 was tuned-up from 0.85 in a prior experiment, we'll regress modestly (+0.001-0.002).

hypothesis: Split Muon weight decay into attn (0.095) vs MLP (0.115) param groups, targeting stronger L2 regularization on MLP matrices which have 2× more parameters per block and are more prone to overfitting in short training runs.
notes: muon_wd_mlp=0.115 for mlp.fc and mlp.proj matrices; muon_wd=0.095 retained for all attention matrices. Implemented as two Muon param groups with per-group weight_decay override. This is the missing piece from the SOTA stack description which mentions split MLP WD but it was absent from the multi_596 baseline code.

hypothesis: Replace cosine LR decay across TTT chunks with constant LR (h.ttt_lr=0.005 throughout), eliminating near-zero adaptation in later chunks which the cosine schedule reduces to ~0.001% of full LR by chunk 90/122.
notes: Cosine decay goes from 0.005 at chunk 0 to ~0 at the last chunks (e.g., chunk 90 of 122 gets LR~0.00073, chunk 110 gets ~0.00007). All chunks equally contribute to BPB, so decaying adaptation hurts later chunks. Constant LR = consistent adaptation throughout. Supported by 'Beyond Cosine Decay' (arxiv 2503.02844, 2025) which shows infinite/flat LR outperforms cosine for continual pre-training. Zero change in compute budget — same number of gradient steps, just at uniform LR=0.005 instead of rapidly decaying.

hypothesis: Switch TTT optimizer from SGD (lr=0.005, momentum=0.9) to AdamW (lr=0.001, betas=(0.9,0.999), wd=0) to leverage adaptive gradient normalization in the ~3-step-per-chunk regime where Adam's second-moment immediately normalizes gradient scale without needing warmup steps.
notes: With chunk_size=32768 and seq_len=2048 on 8 GPUs, each rank sees only 2 sequences per chunk → ~1 gradient step per epoch × 3 epochs = 3 total gradient steps per chunk. In this extreme few-step regime Adam's per-parameter LR normalization is more effective than fixed-LR SGD. The lr default is changed from 0.005→0.001 (standard Adam fine-tuning scale). Cosine-over-chunks schedule retained unchanged.

hypothesis: Add per-head sigmoid output gate to attention (init logit(0.95)≈2.944 for near-identity start), placed after XSA before reshape — lightweight form of NeurIPS 2025 "Gated Attention" (arXiv:2505.06708, also adopted in Qwen3-Next), introducing a bounded non-linearity on the SDPA output.
notes: 88 new scalar params (1D, num_heads per attn layer × 11 layers), routed automatically to scalar_params optimizer via the ndim<2 branch; no CONTROL_TENSOR_NAME_PATTERNS edit required. Init 2.944 → sigmoid≈0.95 so near-identity at step 0 while letting weak heads dampen. The proj is already zero-init so transparency at step 0 is preserved (proj zero × gate = 0). Risk: gate could over-saturate and damp useful heads, but bounded sigmoid prevents runaway. Expected -0.001 to -0.003.

hypothesis: Add learnable per-feature embedding scale (embed_scale, shape=[embedding_dim], init=ones) applied after the input RMSNorm, enabling the model to learn asymmetric weighting between the input and output paths of the tied embedding matrix, with embed_scale also propagating through all x0 residual injections across blocks.
notes: The current embedding RMSNorm has no learnable affine parameters (weight=None). With tied embeddings, tok_emb.weight serves dual roles: input lookup (normalized to unit sphere) and output projection. A per-dim scale after the input RMSNorm lets the model learn which embedding dimensions are most informative at input time (for block attention and x0 residual injection) vs output time (logit projection). The parameter is 512 floats passthrough as float16, ~1KB before compression, compresses to near-zero with brotli-11 (constant-1 array). Added to scalar_params optimizer group (AdamW, lr=scalar_lr=0.02, wd=adam_wd=0.02).

hypothesis: Split Muon weight decay into MLP (0.115) vs attention (0.095) param groups, matching the SOTA stack doc's muon_wd_mlp spec that was not yet implemented in code.
notes: MLP matrices are ~4x larger (2048x512=1M params vs 512x512=262K) and benefit from stronger L2 shrinkage. Two Muon param groups created via list-of-dicts; step() already reads group['weight_decay'] per group so this works without changing Muon internals. No compute cost change.

hypothesis: Frequency-weighted GPTQ Hessian calibration (top-100 tokens get sqrt(2) activation boost → 2× H weight, biasing quantization error toward Zipfian-dominant tokens) plus reduce block_size 128→64 for finer within-block error propagation.
notes: Port of PR #1721 FreqGPTQ technique onto our baseline. Two-pass calibration: first accumulate per-token frequency counts across 64 batches, then replay forward passes with freq-weighted activations in the Hessian hooks. Embedding hook unchanged (captures output activations, not tied to input token IDs). block_size 128→64 is orthogonal and improves error propagation quality. Both changes are zero artifact-size cost.

hypothesis: Within-shard stratification: divide each shard into 4 positional strata (beginning/quarter/half/end), shuffle within each stratum, then interleave so consecutive pops alternate across strata, ensuring ~10-20% of any shard's sequences drawn during training come evenly from all shard positions rather than clustering in one region.
notes: FineWeb shards may have positional structure (web crawl order). Stratified within-shard coverage reduces variance in gradient estimates when only 10-20% of each shard is seen per run. The pop() ordering is reversed so the first sequences drawn alternate across all four quartiles. No shape changes → no recompile cost. Listed as untried in curriculum domain preamble.

hypothesis: Add z-loss auxiliary penalty (1e-4 * logsumexp(logits)^2) to training forward pass; prevents log-partition-function drift complementary to the existing logit_softcap, improving calibration without touching forward_logits or eval paths.
notes: Z-loss from PaLM paper (Chowdhery et al.) applied to post-softcap logits. With softcap=30 bounding logits, logsumexp ranges ~3-15 for a trained model, so z_loss contribution is ~0.001-0.023 (0.03-0.6% of CE). Gradient effect: z_loss pushes all logit magnitudes down at rate ~2e-3*logsumexp*softmax_i, slightly reducing overconfidence. Only forward() is modified; forward_logits() (compiled path used for sliding-window eval and TTT scoring) is untouched. TTT adaptation also uses forward() and will get slight z-loss regularization during adaptation steps.

hypothesis: Split Muon weight decay into attn (0.095) vs MLP (0.115) param groups — resubmit after eval_budget_overrun by environment jitter (3.7s over); training-only change adds no eval time.
notes: Same edit as exp_002: muon_wd_mlp=0.115 for MLP matrices, muon_wd=0.095 for attn matrices. exp_002 showed val_bpb=1.080267 before DQ_EVAL by 3.7s environment jitter. WD split is training-only and does not affect eval runtime.

hypothesis: Shorten Muon momentum warmup fraction from 0.33 → 0.22 to spend ~11% more wallclock at full 0.99 momentum (matching canonical modded-nanogpt fast-ramp schedules).
notes: Meta single-knob tweak on an underexplored axis (LESSONS.md flagged AdamW betas/eps/wd and warmdown_frac as crowded; muon_momentum_warmup_fraction is not on that list). The original 0.33 was a 1500/4550-step ramp; 0.22 ≈ 1000/4550-step ramp, still well above the canonical ~300-step Muon momentum warmup, so early-training stability should be preserved while granting more steps at the high-momentum regime where MuonEq-R does its sharpest updates.

hypothesis: Switch TTT optimizer from SGD (lr=0.005, 3 epochs) to AdamW (lr=0.001, 2 epochs): fewer but higher-quality gradient steps via adaptive normalization, fitting within the eval budget (first attempt with 3 AdamW epochs overran by 3.8s).
notes: First attempt (AdamW/3ep) showed val_bpb=1.080521 but eval_s=603.8s (overrun). Reducing ttt_epochs 3→2 saves ~20s of adaptation compute, bringing eval under 600s. 2 Adam steps with normalized gradients should outperform 3 SGD steps because Adam's second-moment normalization from step 1 makes each update more targeted. lr=0.001 is standard Adam fine-tuning scale vs SGD's 0.005.

hypothesis: FreqGPTQ: two-pass calibration — fast data-only pass counts token frequencies (no model inference), then Hessian collection pass applies sqrt(2) boost to top-100 frequent token activations before H=X^TX; plus block_size 128→64 for finer error propagation.
notes: Fixed eval budget overrun from exp_008: removed batch pre-storage. Now: pass 1 does n_calibration_batches data loads only (no model) to count frequencies, pass 2 does n_calibration_batches normal forward passes with freq-weighted hooks. Total model inference count unchanged vs baseline; overhead is ~64 cheap data loads (~2-3s). Frequency counts from pass 1 are from different (but statistically identical) data vs pass 2 — stable since Zipfian top-100 is corpus-wide.

hypothesis: Bifurcate per-head q_gain into [H,2] — col-0 scales RoPE-rotated head dims, col-1 scales NoPE region — letting the optimizer pick separate temperatures for the positional vs content-only sub-spaces.
notes: Current q_gain=(H,) is broadcast over all 64 head_dims, but only the first rope_dims=16 are RoPE-rotated; remaining 48 are NoPE. RoPE region encodes frequency-decaying local position, NoPE region is content-only — they have qualitatively different attention geometry, so forcing a shared scale is suboptimal. Both cols init at 5.25, so step-0 is identical to baseline; the extra dof only activates as gradients differentiate. Routes to scalar AdamW via existing "q_gain" entry in CONTROL_TENSOR_NAME_PATTERNS (no opt-side change needed). Adds 88 fp32 scalars (8 heads × 1 col × 11 layers); negligible artifact impact.

hypothesis: Replace fixed Muon NS coefficients (3.4445,-4.775,2.0315) with Polar Express per-iteration minimax-optimized tuples for 5 steps, and set min_lr floor to 0.10 to prevent LR collapsing to zero at end of warmdown.
notes: Polar Express NS coefficients from arxiv 2505.16932 give better convergence to the polar factor per NS iteration. MIN_LR=0.10 keeps late-training updates non-trivial during the last ~7% of training (when warmdown_frac=0.72, floor kicks in at frac=0.928). Both techniques validated together in PR #1787 which showed +0.0087 BPB gain over a similar baseline (though that PR also had arch/tok changes). Pure opt contribution estimated -0.002 to -0.004 BPB.

hypothesis: Add a per-feature bigram mixing channel to the embedding: a zero-init [dim] parameter (bigram_scale) that blends the previous token's RMSNorm-normalized embedding into the current token's embedding, giving the model immediate access to bigram context at the input layer.
notes: Bigram subnetworks (Jain et al 2025) show bigram info is critical in first MLP layer; providing it at embedding level shortcuts that learning. Cost: 512 fp16 = 1KB passthrough, negligible size impact. bigram_scale zero-init so starts as no-op baseline, optimizer_scalar (AdamW lr=0.02) updates it during training. torch.cat([zeros_like(x[:,0:1]), x[:,:-1]]) creates a static-shape shifted tensor safe for fullgraph compile.

hypothesis: Add ResFormer-style value residual: cache layer-0 V once, mix `v_mix[0]·V + v_mix[1]·V_layer0` into every later attention's V via a learnable 2-scalar per-block param (init [1.0, 0.0] for step-0 identity); structurally orthogonal to existing XSA / h_gate / bifurcated QK-gain / 3-layer recurrence.
notes: Inspired by Zhou et al. arXiv:2410.17897 (ResFormer/SVFormer) + nanoGPT speedrun learnable-lambda variant where this mechanism delivered ~43% of one record's speedup. CausalSelfAttention.forward now takes optional v_first; layer 0 receives None (constant-folded by torch.compile per call site), captures its V, propagated to all subsequent encoder + decoder layer invocations including the 3-layer recurrence replays of blocks 3-5. Mix happens BEFORE FA3, so XSA's V-projection and the SDPA see the same residualized V. Adds 22 fp32 scalars total (11 layers × 2) — passthrough float16 in GPTQ since numel()<65536, negligible artifact growth (+117 code bytes confirmed). Routes to scalar AdamW via ndim<2 branch — no CONTROL_TENSOR_NAME_PATTERNS edit needed. | torch.distributed.elastic.multiprocessing.errors.ChildFailedError: ============================================================ (train_gpt.py:1441 in step_fn)

hypothesis: Add gentle monotone position-weighted CE: linear ramp from weight 0.8 (pos=0) to 1.2 (pos=T-1), mean-normalized to 1.0, to up-weight later-context predictions and strengthen long-range modeling.
notes: Structural change replacing flat mean-CE with per-token weighted CE. Alpha=0.5 gives a gentle [0.8,1.2] ramp. Mean weight preserved at 1.0 to avoid changing the effective LR. Z-loss unchanged at 1e-4. Compatible with torch.compile(dynamic=False) since T=2048 is a compile-time constant.

hypothesis: TTT per-type LR groups (output proj 3x, input proj 0.5x, others 1x) + momentum 0.95: concentrates adaptation budget on quantized output projections closest to the loss, backed by PR #995 ablation showing this pattern improves short-horizon TTT adaptation.
notes: Three changes: (1) ttt_momentum 0.9→0.95 for smoother gradient accumulation with small batches, (2) per-type SGD param groups with lr_mult stored per group for chunk resets, (3) output proj 3x LR per PR #995 pattern. No HedgeMixer (avoids double-counting with TTT). Eval compute unchanged.

hypothesis: Replace non-overlapping stride (seq_len=2048) with 50% overlapping stride (stride=1024) in shard sampling: 2x more unique starting positions per shard, each token seen from two distinct left-context windows, reducing train/eval stride mismatch and providing richer gradient signal per token within the 600s budget.
notes: Key mechanism: eval uses sliding window stride=64 but training used stride=2048. With stride=1024, 50% of each sequence is a 'cold start' re-prediction with 1-1023 tokens of context, better calibrating the model for early positions in eval windows. No tensor shape changes → no torch.compile retracing issues. 4-strata stratified shuffling preserved within each shard. Implementation: in _reset_shard, stride=max(seq_len//2,1) and num_sequences=(N-S-1-phase)//stride+1.

hypothesis: Split Muon WD (attn=0.095, MLP=0.115) plus reduce gptq_calibration_batches 64→48 to clear the systematic ~603s eval overrun seen in this environment.
notes: Two consecutive DQ_EVALs at 603.6-603.7s show the environment runs ~4s over budget. Reducing calibration batches 64→48 saves ~10s. WD split (muon_wd_mlp=0.115 for MLP, muon_wd=0.095 for attn) is retained from exp_002/011 where pre-TTT val_bpb was 1.0800-1.0799 vs baseline 1.081.

hypothesis: Raise EMA_DECAY 0.9965→0.998 to extend the averaging half-life from ~198 to ~346 steps, capturing more of the mid-late warmdown trajectory where weights are settling under decreasing LR, reducing per-step noise.
notes: Single-knob meta tweak on a less-crowded axis (not in LESSONS.md's wd/beta/eps/warmdown_frac crowded set). Mechanism: with ~3000 step budget and 72% warmdown, half-life=198 averages only last ~10% of cooldown; 346 reaches into the more informative mid-cooldown region without pulling in stale high-LR weights. Same lr_mul shape, no schedule change.

hypothesis: Replace binary top-100 sqrt(2) Hessian boost with continuous sqrt(freq/mean) weighting clamped [0.25,4.0], and reduce GPTQ block_size from 64 to 32 for finer per-block error compensation.
notes: Two complementary calibration improvements: (1) continuous freq weighting is the theoretically optimal Hessian calibration for minimizing BPB (w_i=sqrt(freq_i/mean_freq) exactly matches the contribution of each token to the BPB metric); (2) block_size=32 doubles error-propagation frequency within GPTQ, reducing within-block quantization residuals at zero artifact size cost.

hypothesis: Remove cosine-over-chunks LR decay in TTT: use constant LR=0.005 throughout all chunks instead of decaying to near-zero for later chunks, giving equal adaptation budget to all non-last chunks including the critical tail of the validation set.
notes: AdamW experiment failed (catastrophic forgetting: 1.149 BPB). Reverted to baseline SGD (lr=0.005, momentum=0.9, 3 epochs). Single change: removed the cosine schedule 'cos_lr = ttt_lr*0.5*(1+cos(pi*ci/N))' that set LR to near-zero for later chunks. The last ~20% of adapted chunks previously got LR≈0, contributing almost no adaptation. Constant LR lets all chunks contribute equally, which should better prepare the model for the full val sequence tail.

hypothesis: Polar Express NS coefficients + MIN_LR=0.10 floor (same as exp_016) with ttt_epochs reduced 3→2 to fit within 600s eval budget.
notes: exp_016 showed val_bpb=1.079116 (improvement) but DQ'd at eval_s=603.6s — only 3.6s over budget. Reducing ttt_epochs from 3 to 2 recovers ~60-70s of eval headroom. Expected net: Polar NS + min_lr gains (~-0.003) partially offset by one fewer TTT epoch (~+0.002), resulting in modest improvement vs 1.081 baseline.

hypothesis: Re-submit ResFormer-style value residual with layer-0 v_mix nullified via register_parameter('v_mix', None) so DDP find_unused_parameters=False (default) is happy — layer 0 captures v_first but never consumes one, so its mix param has no gradient path.
notes: Prior submit (exp_018) crashed at ~27s likely because layer 0's `v_mix` never received a gradient (its `if v_first is not None` branch is always False), and DDP at line 1406 uses find_unused_parameters=False so any param without grad triggers ChildFailedError. Fix: after blocks are constructed, `self.blocks[0].attn.register_parameter('v_mix', None)` removes the parameter from layer 0 only; layers 1..10 retain the learnable mix. The forward branch `if v_first is not None` already gates v_mix usage so it correctly skips on layer 0. Code grew +29 bytes; param count drops by 2 vs the previous attempt (no functional change vs that broken version, just DDP-compatibility).

hypothesis: TTT per-type LR groups (3 groups: out_proj 3x, in_proj 0.5x, others 1x) + momentum 0.95 — same technique as prev submit but params batched into 3 groups (not 136) to eliminate per-param overhead that caused 3.5s eval overrun.
notes: Fix for eval_budget_overrun in exp_020: collapsed per-param groups into 3 typed groups (out_proj/in_proj/other) to minimize optimizer overhead. val_bpb from DQ'd run was 1.0799, suggesting direction is valid.

hypothesis: Add normalized focal loss (γ=0.5) on top of position ramp: downweights easy tokens (high p_correct), upweights hard tokens, normalized to mean=1 to preserve gradient scale, addressing token-difficulty bias orthogonally to the positional ramp.
notes: p_t = exp(-ce.detach()); focal_w = (1-p_t)^0.5 / mean; ce_loss = mean(per_tok_ce * pos_w * focal_w). Keeps z-loss at 1e-4 unchanged. The normalization ensures total gradient magnitude matches plain CE, while redistributing it toward harder tokens.

hypothesis: Sequence-length warmup curriculum: train at seq_len=512 for first 30% of wallclock (faster steps due to O(L²) attention savings, ~10-20% more gradient steps), then switch to seq_len=2048 for the remainder; both graph shapes pre-compiled during warmup to avoid in-budget recompile overhead.
notes: SkyLadder-style short-to-long context schedule. At seq_len=512 with same train_batch_tokens=786432, attention compute per token is 4x lower (2*L*D vs 2*2048*D per token), so each training step is ~15-20% faster. Pre-compile at seq_len=512 happens during warmup (before t0) so no recompile cost inside training budget. Transition at frac=0.30 to train_seq_len=2048 (already compiled). Expected ~10% more effective gradient steps total. | torch.distributed.elastic.multiprocessing.errors.ChildFailedError: ============================================================ (train_gpt.py:1170 in eval_val)

hypothesis: Add a learned per-feature word-boundary bias (word_boundary_scale, zero-init [D]) applied only to SP8192 word-start tokens (▁ prefix), injecting explicit morphological structure into the embedding pipeline that the model would otherwise need to infer from token IDs.
notes: SP8192 tokens starting with ▁ (leading space) mark word boundaries. The model must currently infer this from token identities; injecting a learned zero-init feature vector for boundary tokens gives a direct shortcut and frees embedding capacity. The has_boundary_lut is a persistent bool buffer built from the tokenizer in GPT.__init__ (8192 entries, 8KB, ~1KB compressed). word_boundary_scale is a [512] AdamW scalar param. Total artifact size addition: ~1.5KB. The deserialize() path recreates GPT(h) so the LUT is rebuilt correctly at eval time. Inspired by PR #1252 WARP word-boundary injection technique.

hypothesis: Add stochastic depth regularization with linear schedule (drop_path_prob linearly from 0% at layer 0 to 10% at layer 10), applied per-sample during training only, to provide implicit ensemble diversity and faster gradient flow through shorter effective paths.
notes: No existing dropout/stochastic-depth in baseline. Linear schedule is canonical (Huang 2016) — early layers get near-zero drop so gradient signal is preserved for the undertrained regime. 10% max is conservative to avoid hurting the model in the ~600s training budget. Looped layers 3-5 get independently-drawn masks on each pass, which is correct behavior.

hypothesis: Replace binary top-100 sqrt(2) Hessian boost with continuous sqrt(freq/mean) weighting clamped [0.25,4.0]; block_size stays at 64 to avoid eval budget overrun.
notes: Continuous freq weighting is theoretically optimal: w_i=sqrt(freq_i/mean_freq) makes the Hessian minimize expected BPB error weighted by actual token occurrence frequency, vs the previous binary all-or-nothing top-100 boost. Zero eval-time overhead since it only changes the fw_tensor computation before the calibration forward passes. block_size reverted to 64 after prior trial (exp_024) had 3.5s eval overrun from 2x Cholesky ops.

hypothesis: Move enable_looping_at 0.35 → 0.22 so the looped (eval-matched) depth-recurrence architecture gets ~6% of high-LR training before warmdown starts at frac=0.28, instead of activating only after warmdown has begun decaying the LR.
notes: Meta sweep on a fresh knob avoiding the crowded beta/eps/wd/warmdown_frac territory flagged in LESSONS.md. Mechanism: align eval-architecture (looped) with peak-LR training phase. Trade-off: ~6% fewer total steps (looped is ~1.55× slower per step), but those steps train the architecture used at inference. No structural changes; single hyperparameter shift.

hypothesis: Constant LR throughout TTT chunks (remove cosine decay) with 2 SGD epochs: uniform adaptation budget across all chunks instead of decaying to zero for late chunks, with epoch reduction to fit eval budget (baseline SGD/3ep cosine is right at the 600s edge).
notes: Both AdamW/3ep and SGD-constant/3ep overran at exactly 603.8s, confirming baseline is at the budget edge. Total adaptation budget analysis: cosine/3ep sum-of-LRs ≈ 0.5*N*ttt_lr; constant/2ep = 1.0*N*ttt_lr — so this delivers 2x as much total gradient signal across the full val sequence, distributed uniformly rather than front-loaded. Trade-off: 1 fewer epoch but equal weight to ALL chunks (earlier cosine gave near-zero to last 20% of chunks).

hypothesis: Lower min_lr from 0.10 to 0.02: eliminates the 43s flat-LR tail at end of warmdown (where LR was clamped at 10% of peak), enabling deeper final convergence aligned with linear-decay-to-zero (D2Z) research showing this schedule is optimal.
notes: arxiv 2502.15938 shows linear D2Z consistently outperforms cosine and other schedules. With warmdown_frac=0.72 and old min_lr=0.10, LR was stuck at 10% for last 7.2% of budget (~43s). With min_lr=0.02, LR continues decaying to 2% floor (only 1.4% of budget stuck at floor). This allows the EMA to accumulate a more deeply converged model state. EMA decay=0.9965 unchanged.

hypothesis: Add label smoothing ε=0.1 to CE: prevents overconfidence on easy tokens without redistributing gradient between positions, complementary to the existing z-loss (logit-scale) and position ramp (positional-bias) regularization.
notes: Focal loss (prior trial exp_029) hurt BPB by 0.008 — gradient redistribution away from common tokens creates train/eval mismatch. Label smoothing is structurally different: uniform entropy regularization, no per-token gradient redistribution. Adds near-zero overhead (handled in PyTorch C++ backend).

hypothesis: Apply cosine LR decay across TTT epochs within each chunk: epoch 0 gets full LR, epoch 1 gets 0.75×, epoch 2 gets 0.25×, preventing SGD momentum overshoot in later epochs and yielding a more stably converged adapted model per chunk.
notes: Removed the per-chunk constant LR reset block and moved LR updates inside the epoch loop with cosine decay: cos_scale = 0.5*(1+cos(pi*ep/max(epochs,1))). Zero compute overhead, pure scheduling improvement for 3-epoch TTT adaptation per 32K-token chunk.

hypothesis: Add learned per-feature word-boundary bias (word_boundary_scale, zero-init [D]) for SP8192 word-start tokens; LUT populated from val_data.sp in train_model() so deserialize() adds no tokenizer load overhead.
notes: Fix for exp_031 eval_budget_overrun: tokenizer loading was happening inside GPT.__init__ which is called twice by deserialize() during eval. Now GPT.__init__ only registers a zero bool buffer (no I/O); train_model() populates it from the already-loaded val_data.sp. deserialize() creates GPT(h) with zero placeholder then load_state_dict overwrites with correct serialized values. No extra tokenizer load in eval path.

hypothesis: Add RWKV-style per-channel learnable token-shift (smear) that blends the previous-position hidden state into each block's pre-attention input via a sigmoid gate, init logit -3.0 (~5% prev-token), giving attention free position-1 context that complements partial-RoPE.
notes: Per-block nn.Parameter shape (dim,) = 512 fp32, 11 layers → 5632 params (~22KB raw, ~11KB fp16-passthrough). Routes to scalar AdamW via ndim<2 branch (no CONTROL_TENSOR edit). F.pad((0,0,1,0)) preserves causality (pos 0 sees zero). Affects both sublayers in parallel-residual mode (layers 7-10) and only attn input in sequential mode (layers 0-6). RWKV uses TokenShift as core feature; nanoGPT speedrun smear mod is similar; both report meaningful gains at small scale.

hypothesis: Mask the first 25% of each training sequence (positions 0 to seq_len//4-1) from the cross-entropy loss, focusing gradient signal on positions with ≥512 tokens of context and reducing high-entropy zero-context gradient noise.
notes: Loss masking via positional curriculum: positions 0-511 in a 2048-token sequence have minimal left-context and produce high-variance, low-signal gradients (predicting first tokens with no context). Masking them focuses training on positions 512-2047 (75% of sequence), better aligned with sliding-window eval where most tokens are scored with 1984 tokens of context. The final primary metric (quantized_ttt, eval_val_ttt) uses forward_logits directly — not GPT.forward — so it is unaffected by this mask. No shape changes, no recompile cost. The seq_len//4 division is a compile-time constant under torch.compile(dynamic=False) so fullgraph=True is preserved.

hypothesis: Add 2% random token replacement noise to training inputs (token-level augmentation), forcing the model to use broader context rather than local token copying, complementing existing stochastic depth regularization without reducing gradient signal.
notes: Token noise applied outside the compiled model (in training loop before model(x,y)), so no torch.compile interaction. 2% rate corrupts ~41 tokens per 2048-length sequence. Mechanism: causal LM is forced to predict correct y from occasionally-wrong x[t-1], encouraging context use. Rate chosen analogous to BERT's 1.5% random replacement. Avoids standard dropout (shown harmful for single-epoch pretraining by arxiv 2505.24788).

hypothesis: Lower enable_looping_at 0.22→0.15: engage 3-layer depth recurrence ~42s earlier so looped layers receive ~9% more wallclock at full effective depth, trading ~10% raw step count for richer training of the looped circuit.
notes: Schedule-fraction knob (clean meta-domain), distinct from crowded warmdown_frac/AdamW axes. Comment in code records prior shortening 0.33→0.22 with positive result; this probes whether the optimum lies further down. enable_looping_at and muon_momentum_warmup_fraction are intentionally decoupled here — momentum still finishes warming at frac=0.22 as before. Looped graph is pre-compiled during warmup_steps so no torch.compile recompile penalty mid-training.

hypothesis: Increase ttt_epochs 2→4 with cosine LR decay within each chunk's adaptation (lr anneals from ttt_lr at epoch 0 to ~15% of ttt_lr at epoch 3), restoring the SOTA-stack epoch count and preventing oscillation in later passes.
notes: SOTA stack baseline used ttt_epochs=4; current best at 2 epochs is likely under-adapting per chunk. Cosine decay within epochs (full lr epoch 0, ~15% lr epoch 3) prevents oscillation in later passes. With warmdown_frac=0.58, LAWA collects ~40 snapshots; without floor, last ~4 snapshots are at near-zero LR and are nearly identical — wasting diversity. Floor at 0.10 hits at frac≈0.942, keeping final snapshots at useful LR. Linear D2Z is claimed optimal in isolation, but LAWA averaging changes the incentive to maintain snapshot diversity.

hypothesis: Replace SGD with AdamW (lr=5e-4, wd=0, betas=(0.9,0.999)) in the TTT inner loop; AdamW's per-parameter adaptive gradient scaling should converge more efficiently than uniform-LR SGD on the quantized model's heterogeneous gradient magnitudes across layers within the same 3-epoch budget.
notes: ttt_lr default changed from 0.005 to 0.0005 (10x reduction appropriate for AdamW vs SGD). Cosine per-epoch schedule unchanged so LR still decays from 5e-4 to 5e-5 over 3 epochs. freeze_layers=5, chunk_tokens=32768, ttt_epochs=3 all unchanged. Only the optimizer family switches.

hypothesis: Reduce GPTQ block_size from 64 to 32 for twice-as-frequent cross-block Hessian error compensation, targeting the wide 2048-column MLP projection matrices where accumulated quantization error between corrections is highest.
notes: GPTQ block_size controls how often residual quantization errors are propagated to remaining columns via the inverse Hessian. Smaller blocks = more frequent correction = less accumulated error. Wide MLP matrices (2048 cols) benefit most. No artifact size impact. Current sigma tuning (12.85 MLP, 10.0 attn) and freq-weighted Hessians already present; this targets a different axis (error propagation granularity). Expected ~0.001-0.003 BPB improvement from finer residual updates.

hypothesis: Remove Canon-B (per-Q/K/V depthwise convs) inside attention — the existing Canon-A + Canon-C + Canon-D + shift_mix stack already saturates the horizontal-context axis at our 30M undertrained scale, and Canon-B on raw post-projection Q/K/V double-dips with Canon-A on the same data path; expect cleaner FA3 input and freed gradient capacity.
notes: Drops 4*(dim+2*kv_dim) = 4096 fp32 train params per layer (~180 KB across 11 blocks pre-quant; ~50-90 KB artifact). Three fewer Canon residual ops per layer in forward. Zero-init residual structure → ablation is structural, not just a magnitude tweak. Reverts only Canon-B; A/C/D + shift_mix retained. | torch.distributed.elastic.multiprocessing.errors.ChildFailedError: ============================================================ (train_gpt.py:1123 in forward)

hypothesis: Add 10%-of-peak floor to TTT cosine LR so the late-chunk tail keeps drift-correcting instead of freezing (current schedule drops below 10% of peak by ci≈80/100 and below 1% by ci≈95).
notes: Surgical TTT-side meta tweak. Peak ttt_lr unchanged (still 0.005), only the cosine floor changes from 0 to 0.1·ttt_lr. Should add zero compute (same SGD step count, just non-vanishing scale on late steps). Not in the AdamW-beta/eps/wd/warmdown crowded-knob set.

hypothesis: Replace independent per-token noise with DropBlock-style contiguous 8-token span masking at the same 2% average rate, forcing the model to use longer-range context within masked spans.
notes: token_noise_block=8: each corrupted block is 8 consecutive tokens; block drop probability = token_noise_prob so expected fraction masked is identical to independent case (2%). Mechanism: within a masked span, positions 1-7 see only corrupted left context in the causal window, forcing reliance on pre-span context >8 tokens back. Warmdown coupling unchanged — noise decays to 0 with stoch_depth_scale during warmdown. Addresses DropBlock-style structured mask introduction noted in reg domain.

hypothesis: Stride annealing during warmdown (seq_len//2 → seq_len//4) plus Van der Corput base-2 phase selection for uniform shard-reset coverage, reducing train/eval stride gap as weights converge.
notes: During warmdown (frac > 0.28), stride linearly narrows from 1024 to 512 (min seq_len//4), providing denser position coverage matching eval's stride=64 behavior. VdC phase replaces random phase-per-reset with a deterministic low-discrepancy sequence for perfectly uniform starting-position coverage across shard resets. training_frac attribute updated each step from main loop so _reset_shard uses current time position.

hypothesis: Freeze per-head/per-channel control gain scalars (attn_scale, mlp_scale, resid_mix, q_gain, skip_weights, skip_gates) during TTT so only weight matrices adapt — prevents tiny tensors from overfitting to local chunk distributions (replicates exp_491 mechanism on new stack).
notes: exp_491 on old 1.081 stack kept with delta=-0.000043. Mechanism: control gain tensors (8-1536 elements) can overfit to 32K-token chunks during TTT SGD; freezing them focuses adaptation budget on large weight matrices that carry real domain-shift signal. No compute change. Polyak/save/restore only cover trainable params now.

hypothesis: Add fourgram (t-3) embedding channel with zero-init per-feature scale, and initialize logit_bias from SentencePiece piece scores as a unigram prior for faster early convergence.
notes: Fourgram extends the existing bigram/trigram shortcut channels one step further: zero-init so it starts transparent and can be pruned by the optimizer if unhelpful. Logit bias init from SP scores shifts the model's initial output distribution toward the corpus unigram distribution, reducing the initial loss and letting early gradient steps focus on conditional patterns rather than learning the unigram prior from scratch. Both changes add negligible artifact cost (fourgram_scale is 512 fp32 scalars; logit_bias artifact size is unchanged since the artifact stores final trained values).

hypothesis: Reduce min_lr from 0.10 to 0.0 (linear decay-to-zero), motivated by arxiv:2502.15938 showing D2Z consistently outperforms linear decay to 10% — the exact schedule we run currently.
notes: The D2Z paper (arxiv 2502.15938) shows linear decay to zero outperforms 10x-decay schedules (including cosine-to-10% and linear-to-10%) by explaining that low final LR enables better implicit averaging over late-training updates — exactly what LAWA is doing explicitly. With warmdown_frac=0.58 and ~40 LAWA snapshots, the last several snapshots at near-zero LR represent the most converged model state and should improve the LAWA average. With min_lr=0.0, at t=1.0 the Muon and AdamW updates are both no-ops (lr=0 means no gradient step, no weight decay), so no numerical risk.

hypothesis: Raise attn_clip_sigmas from 10.0 to 12.0 so attention matrices (Q, K, V, O) use the same loose SDClip as MLP, preserving high-magnitude outliers in Q/K weights amplified by the QK-gain (5.25 init).
notes: PR #1769 showed MLP sigma 10→12 helps preserve outliers; our MLP is already at 12.85 but attention was still at 10.0. With QK-Gain init=5.25, the Q and K weight matrices can develop high-magnitude rows that 10σ clips. Widening to 12σ should preserve those outliers in the int6 quantization grid. No eval time impact (only affects the SDClip scalar calculation, not the GPTQ loop). First submit (block_size=64→32) overran eval budget by 3.3s; this submit uses same block_size=64 baseline.

hypothesis: Add label smoothing eps=0.1 to the CE loss: redistributes 10% of target mass uniformly, which per neural-collapse literature (ICLR 2025) accelerates convergence to well-separated feature representations—valuable in the severely undertrained 600s budget.
notes: exp_075 uses plain uniform CE (no position weighting, no smoothing). Label smoothing eps=0.1: each correct token's target drops from 1.0 to 0.9, with 0.1/V=1.2e-5 added to each wrong token. Gradient on correct logit: magnitude reduced by ~10% relative to plain CE. Theoretical motivation: lower conditioning number + faster neural collapse convergence (Guo et al., ICLR 2025). Risk: if the 10% gradient reduction on correct tokens outweighs convergence acceleration, BPB degrades ~+0.002.

hypothesis: Set min_lr=0.1 (Chinchilla-style 10x decay floor) so the final ~7.2% of warmdown plateaus at 10% base LR instead of decaying to zero — under our undertrained 600s budget the D2Z tail wastes signal.
notes: Single-knob meta tweak: min_lr 0.0 → 0.1. lr_mul = max((1-frac)/warmdown_frac, min_lr); with warmdown_frac=0.72, floor activates at frac > 0.928. Motivation: arxiv 2502.15938 ("Straight to Zero") finds D2Z optimal only at compute-optimal training; our regime is severely undertrained so a non-zero floor preserves tail-end learning. min_lr is the only LR-schedule knob LESSONS does NOT flag as over-explored.

hypothesis: Remove Canon-D from MLP: inside-MLP kernel-4 depthwise conv on wide hidden=2048 has 8192 zero-init fp32 taps per layer that struggle to populate in our 6000-step undertrained regime; canon_a/canon_c on dim=512 already provide horizontal-context flow at 4x higher per-tap gradient flux. Following Canon-B removal precedent.
notes: Architectural subtraction. Step-0 identity preserved (canon_d was zero-init). Saves ~352 KB train params plus 22-50 KB artifact. If positive, follow-up tests removing canon_a OR shift_mix one at a time.

hypothesis: Switch TTT optimizer from AdamW to SGD with Nesterov momentum (momentum=0.9, lr=0.005) — cold-started AdamW is near-sign-gradient in the 3-step per-chunk regime, while Nesterov SGD takes full-size steps from step 0 with the lookahead gradient, matching effective step sizes and converging faster in the short adaptation window.
notes: AdamW(lr=0.0005) → SGD(lr=0.005, momentum=0.9, nesterov=True). The ttt_momentum=0.9 hyperparameter already existed but was unused. With state.clear() between chunks, each chunk gets fresh momentum, but Nesterov's lookahead evaluation helps within each 3-step epoch sequence. 10x LR increase compensates for SGD lacking adaptive normalisation.

hypothesis: Anneal warmdown training stride aggressively from 1024 to 64 (matching eval stride=64) instead of stopping at 512, using slope (1-wd_prog) instead of (1-0.5*wd_prog), to close the train/eval stride distribution gap during model convergence.
notes: Current warmdown stride goes 1024→512. Eval uses stride=64 (8x gap). By annealing to min_s=64 with steeper slope (1-wd_prog vs 1-0.5*wd_prog), stride reaches 64 at wd_prog=0.9375 (last ~6% of warmdown). This aligns training distribution with sliding-window eval regime, consistent with ACL 2025 finding that overlapping context training improves next-token prediction diversity. Warmdown_frac=0.72 means warmdown covers 432s of 600s; the small-stride phase covers the final ~26s of training.

hypothesis: Extend WD taper to token embedding AdamW optimizer (was only applied to Muon matrices): tied embeddings taper from embed_wd=0.085 to 0.0425 during final convergence (frac 0.70→1.00), letting the logit/embedding weights settle to a lower minimum without L2 restraint fighting final convergence.
notes: WD taper currently only covers optimizer_muon.param_groups. Added base_weight_decay tracking to optimizer_tok and apply wd_scale in the main loop. Since embeddings are tied with lm_head, this reduces the L2 pull on the most directly BPB-relevant parameters during warmdown convergence. No change to architecture, training budget, or eval.

hypothesis: Freeze control gain scalars (attn_scale, mlp_scale, resid_mix, q_gain, skip_weights, skip_gates) before torch.compile in TTT so dynamo sees final requires_grad state at trace time and never retraces mid-eval, fixing the eval_budget_overrun from the prior attempt.
notes: Prior attempt (exp_128) hit eval_budget_overrun by 4.2s due to dynamo retrace triggered by requires_grad_(False) on frozen params AFTER compile. Fix: move requires_grad setup before torch.compile so compiled graph has consistent param state. Control scalar freeze itself: per exp_491 on old stack (delta=-0.000043), prevents tiny gain tensors (q_gain=8 elem, attn_scale/mlp_scale=512 elem/block) from overfitting to chunk-local distributions during TTT SGD.

hypothesis: Add fivegram_scale channel: pure normalized token embedding at t-4 (computed before any channel modifications), zero-initialized, giving the model an independent direct handle on 4-step-back context that is currently only accessible in entangled form inside the mixed fourgram channel.
notes: The existing fourgram_emb is computed from the MODIFIED x (after bigram/trigram additions), implicitly containing t-4 signal mixed with other scales. The new fivegram uses the PURE normalized embedding at t-4, providing an orthogonal, independently-gated channel. Zero-init ensures transparency at start. Parameter cost: 512 float32 scalars (~1KB passthrough float16 → negligible vs artifact budget).

hypothesis: Replace linear LR warmdown with cosine warmdown: lr = min_lr + (1-min_lr) * 0.5*(1+cos(π*t)), giving higher LR in early warmdown for more effective gradient steps and smoother convergence at the tail.
notes: Cosine warmdown keeps LR ~10-15% higher than linear through the first 80% of warmdown, then drops faster. With warmdown_frac=0.58 and min_lr=0.10, linear hits the floor at t=0.90 while cosine approaches it smoothly. LAWA snapshots collected across warmdown benefit from earlier high-LR checkpoints. This is a pure schedule-shape change with no architecture or quantization changes.

hypothesis: Reduce GPTQ block_size from 64 to 32 so each block's Hessian error propagates at finer granularity, reducing accumulated quantization error per weight column.
notes: INIT.md seed stack describes block_size=32 as baseline; the code had default=64 with no override at the call site, so the baseline may have been running at the wrong block size. block_size=32 doubles the number of error-propagation steps during GPTQ, reducing within-block error accumulation. No size change (only affects quant quality). Combined with existing freq-weighted Hessians and matrix_clip_sigmas=12.85 / attn_clip_sigmas=12.0.

hypothesis: Lower min_lr from 0.1 to 0.05 — halves the LR floor during the last ~7% of warmdown so the EMA endpoint absorbs less late-stage SGD noise.
notes: Fresh axis (warmdown_frac/betas/eps/wd are crowded per LESSONS, min_lr is not). Modded-nanogpt prior record tested min_lr=0.05 with marginal positive impact. Total LR-time changes from 0.6445 to 0.6420 (≈0.4% reduction); shape of decay near t=1 is the meaningful difference, since that region dominates the EMA tail at decay=0.999.

hypothesis: Add gentle linear position weighting (0.5→1.5, mean=1.0) to training CE loss, aligning gradient emphasis with sliding-window eval where only the last stride (64/2048) positions are scored per window.
notes: eval_val_ttt also calls base_model(x,y) so TTT adaptation inherits the position weighting, further focusing adaptation on later positions matching the score-first eval regime. label_smoothing=0.1 retained. torch.linspace(0.5,1.5,T) used for compile compatibility.

hypothesis: Restrict value-residual v_mix to top 4 layers (7-10) per ResFormer ACL'25 ablation; nullify v_mix on layers 0-6 where the paper reports v_first blending degrades early-layer generalization.
notes: ResFormer (arXiv:2410.17897, ACL 2025) Table-3/§4.4 finds best v-residual placement = top 3 of 8 layers (~37% depth); extending earlier "diminishes the overall effect". Scaled to our 11-layer stack: keep v_mix on layers 7-10 (top 4 ≈ 36%), nullify on 0-6. Step-0 forward bit-identical to baseline (kept layers retain init [1.0, 0.0]; nullified layers' pre-edit branch computed v = 1.0*v + 0.0*v_first ≡ v). Only the gradient path changes — early v_mix[1] stops receiving gradient, concentrating residual benefit where ResFormer's Learnable-ResFormer empirically pushes lambda. Drops 12 fp32 train params (~48 bytes), negligible. Compile-friendly: `self.v_mix is None` is a stable Python attribute check, specializes per Block instance.

hypothesis: Apply Newton-Schulz gradient normalization (Muon-style) to 2D weight gradients in the TTT inner loop, replacing the clip_grad_norm_(1.0) that would otherwise nullify NS-normalized updates; this gives scale-invariant per-layer updates matching the LaCT paper finding that Muon outperforms momentum SGD for TTT.
notes: LaCT (arxiv 2505.23884, ICLR 2026) shows Muon consistently outperforms SGD+momentum for TTT. Our codebase has zeropower_via_newtonschulz5 already compiled. Applying it to 2D param grads before SGD step normalizes spectral scale across all adapted layers. Removed clip_grad_norm_(1.0) because NS-normalized grads have total Frobenius norm ~166 (42 adapted matrices * sqrt(512) each), which the 1.0 clip would reduce by factor 166x effectively zeroing all updates. Per-element update magnitude with NS is bounded at ~LR*0.044 which is safe without explicit clipping.

hypothesis: Exclude control gain scalars (attn_scale, mlp_scale, resid_mix, q_gain, skip_weights, skip_gates) from TTT optimizer groups without changing requires_grad, so dynamo graph is identical to baseline (cache hit) but SGD never updates the tiny per-head/per-channel gains that overfit to chunk-local distributions.
notes: Prior attempts (exp_128, exp_138) used requires_grad_(False) which changes dynamo tensor guards and causes a compile cache miss (~10-15s recompile overhead) that pushed eval over 600s by 4s. This version keeps requires_grad=True for all params (identical graph to baseline) and achieves the same freeze effect via optimizer exclusion only. Gradient is computed but silently discarded for the frozen params (negligible overhead: ~25K/10M elements). Mechanism: control gain scalars (8-1536 elements each) can overfit to 32K-token chunk distributions; letting only weight matrices adapt is cleaner.

hypothesis: Add per-layer attention output dropout (linear schedule 0→2% across layers, warmdown-coupled via stoch_depth_scale) as a complementary regularizer to the existing MLP dropout — a mechanism swap from 0→nonzero on the attention pathway.
notes: attn_drop_max=0.02, applied element-wise after flash-attention output, linear 0→max across layers 0→10, decays to 0 during warmdown (stoch_depth_scale). Complements MLP drop (0.02 global / 0.05 loop) and stoch depth (0→10%). No change to model size or architecture.

hypothesis: Replace VDC base-2 phase with golden-ratio sequence (optimal 1D coverage) and switch warmdown stride annealing from linear to cosine (keeps larger/diverse stride early in warmdown when LR is still high, then rapidly converges to eval stride=64 at the end).
notes: Two complementary curriculum improvements: (1) Golden ratio phi^-1=0.618... replaces VDC base-2 for shard phase generation — avoids binary-tree aliasing and gives optimal discrepancy for any N resets. (2) Cosine stride schedule during warmdown: stride = max(64, int(half * 0.5 * (1+cos(pi*wd_prog)))) vs old linear max(64, int(half*(1-wd_prog))). Cosine decays slower early (more data diversity when LR is high) then faster late (aligns with eval stride sooner). Rationale: during early warmdown, model still makes large LR updates and benefits from diverse data; toward the end, converging to eval-like stride=64 matters more for final distribution alignment.

hypothesis: Add post-blend RMSNorm before x0 = x to normalize the full multi-channel embedding (bigram/trigram/fourgram/fivegram/boundary) to unit scale, giving every block's resid_mix injection a consistent skip-connection baseline.
notes: Zero parameter cost; identity at init (embed_scale=1, all other channels zero-init → post-blend x is already ~unit-norm). During training, channels activate and push x scale away from 1; second RMSNorm prevents drift. The resid_mix[1] parameters throughout the network can then learn clean scale-agnostic x0 injection.

hypothesis: Revert block_size to 64 (fixing timing issue from prior trial) and halve GPTQ Hessian damping from 0.001 to 0.0005, reducing regularization bias in the Cholesky inverse for more accurate weight compensation.
notes: Prior trial (block_size=32) got eval_budget_overrun and worse val_bpb, likely due to slower GPTQ hitting the outer training timeout. This trial reverts to block_size=64 (safe timing) and applies only a compute-neutral change: halving damping from 0.001 to 0.0005 for lower Hessian regularization bias (following PR #1318 insight). With freq-weighted Hessians and 48 calibration batches, Hessian conditioning should be good enough to support lower damping.

hypothesis: Bump tied_embed_init_std 0.010 → 0.014 to reduce flat-logit suppression of early embedding learning under tied weights.
notes: Under-explored axis (LESSONS noted opt/wd/beta crowding, but init_std rarely touched). Tied embedding receives gradient from input + output paths, so init=0.010 keeps logits flatter than necessary at step 0; 0.014 is a midpoint toward modded-nanogpt defaults (~0.02) without risking instability. No size impact.

hypothesis: Add Muon+ post-NS col-row normalization: after Newton-Schulz, normalize columns then rows (Muon+ arxiv 2602.21545), keeping existing pre-NS row-norm (MuonEq-R). Balances both input-feature and output-neuron gradient scales post-orthogonalization.
notes: Muon+ paper shows 1.7-2.0 point perplexity improvements on GPT-Small/Base vs vanilla Muon. We already have pre-NS row-norm (MuonEq-R); adding post-NS col-row norm is additive. The col-norm equalizes per-input-feature gradient magnitude; the subsequent row-norm re-equalizes after col-norm perturbs row balance. All in bf16 with fp32 norm computation for numerical stability.

hypothesis: Add PaLM-style z-loss (weight=1e-4): z_loss = 1e-4 * mean(logsumexp(logits)^2) encourages moderate logit magnitudes and better-calibrated distributions, complementing existing label_smoothing=0.1 even under logit softcap.
notes: Z-loss added as additive term to forward(). logsumexp over float logits (post-softcap, bounded in [-30,30]). Typical z contribution ~9-15 for well-trained distributions; weight 1e-4 adds ~0.5-1% of CE to total loss. Complementary to label_smoothing which penalizes entropy; z-loss penalizes partition function magnitude directly.

hypothesis: Remove optimizer.state.clear() between TTT chunks so Nesterov SGD momentum warm-starts each chunk from the previous chunk's accumulated gradient direction; FineWeb validation is sequential text so consecutive 32K chunks are correlated and the 0.9^3≈0.73 natural decay gives ~3 chunks of useful gradient memory with no extra compute.
notes: First trial (NS normalization) hurt due to gradient magnitude being informative for TTT and caused eval overrun. This second trial reverts NS, restores clip_grad_norm_(1.0), and only removes state.clear(). Mechanism: sequential validation text means chunk N's optimal adaptation direction is correlated with chunk N+1. Warm-start momentum provides inertia in the right direction without preventing new-chunk adaptation (0.9^3 per-step decay = ~0.73 remaining after 3 gradient steps). Zero compute overhead vs baseline.

hypothesis: Make XSA subtraction strength input-dependent: add per-layer zero-init (num_heads,12) projection from the first 12 residual channels of the post-norm attention input, summed with the existing per-head static xsa_lambda before the self-projection subtraction; mirrors the validated attn_gate dynamic-delta pattern, lets λ scale with attention-sink risk per token instead of collapsing to one per-head compromise.
notes: Step-0 identity preserved (zero-init weight → λ ≡ xsa_lambda init=1.0, bit-identical to Apple XSA). Reuses existing 12-channel slice from attn_gate; weights are decoupled. Cost: 96 fp32/layer (~4KB train), fp16+brotli artifact ≪1KB. _xsa_efficient signature changed to take precomputed (B,T,H) λ tensor; broadcast reshaped to (B,T,Hkv,group,1) for GQA layout.

hypothesis: Extend stride annealing to the full training run via two-stage cosine: pre-warmdown cosines stride from seq_len=2048 (zero overlap, max diversity) down to seq_len//2=1024, joining continuously the existing warmdown cosine from 1024 to 64, so the model progressively closes the training-eval distribution gap throughout all of training.
notes: Current baseline uses fixed stride=seq_len//2=1024 pre-warmdown, then cosine 1024→64 during warmdown. This change adds a first-stage cosine that takes stride from 2048→1024 during pre-warmdown (ws=0.28 of training). Both stages are monotonically decreasing and join at frac=ws where both give stride=1024. Early training sees zero token-window overlap (unique windows), which maximizes data diversity; as training progresses overlap increases, reaching the eval distribution (stride=64) at the end of warmdown.

hypothesis: Extend WD taper window earlier (start_frac 0.70→0.55) and deepen it (final_mult 0.50→0.15) so weight decay tapers to near-zero during the dominant warmdown convergence phase, allowing tighter gradient-direction fitting when LR is still meaningful.
notes: WD taper currently only active in final 30% of training; at taper-start LR is 42% of base. Pushing taper start to 55% means WD begins reducing when LR is still 63% of base, giving more of the warmdown phase reduced-WD convergence. Final mult 0.15 (vs 0.50) means weights can settle closer to gradient optimum at end of training without WD shrinkage fighting the direction. Historical wd-to-zero failures were full-training, not warmdown-only.

hypothesis: Change polyak_decay in eval_val_ttt from 0.99 to 0.9 so the Polyak-averaged scoring model incorporates ~27% of TTT-adapted weights per chunk (vs ~3% at 0.99), meaningfully exploiting TTT adaptation in early chunks where the EMA was previously indistinguishable from the initial model.
notes: With 3 gradient steps per GPU per chunk (chunk_seqs=16 seqs / 8 GPUs = 2 seqs, batch_seqs=32 → 1 step/epoch × 3 epochs), polyak_decay=0.99 leaves 97% of the initial model in the Polyak state. At decay=0.9, ~27% of adapted weights feed into the scoring model. The last TTT epoch runs at 0.25× LR (cosine decay), so final-step params are stable — no oscillation risk. Zero compute added.

hypothesis: Reduce GPTQ block_size from 64 to 32, enabling more frequent Hessian-error-propagation passes per weight matrix and tighter quantization residuals without affecting artifact size or training.
notes: Current code uses block_size=64 (function default). INIT.md seed-stack (multi_596) used block_size=32. Smaller block_size strictly improves GPTQ quantization quality: each column's rounding error is compensated more frequently across the remaining columns (2x more propagation steps for 512-dim attn, 4x for 2048-dim MLP fc). Artifact size unchanged — only quantized weight values change, not their storage format. Expected ~0.001-0.003 val_bpb improvement from tighter int6 grids.

hypothesis: Set MIN_LR floor 0.05 → 0.0 (D2Z, arxiv 2502.15938) so the linear warmdown decays straight to zero in the final ~3.6% of wallclock instead of being held at a 5% floor.
notes: Bergsma+Dey 2025 ("Straight to Zero") report that linear decay-to-zero consistently outperforms decay-to-10x-floor at compute-optimal sizes. With warmdown_frac=0.72 the 5% floor only applies in the final ~3.6% of training (≈last 22s of wallclock); removing it lets Muon's 0.99-momentum updates settle into a basin while leaving peak LR, warmup, batch, momentum, WD untouched. Touches a meta axis not present in the prior beta/eps/wd/warmdown_frac crowded set.

hypothesis: Add trigram×fourgram interaction channel (t-3, t-2 token pair element-wise product) to the embedding pipeline, mirroring the existing bigram×trigram interaction but shifted one step back, giving the model explicit access to the (t-3,t-2) co-occurrence pattern at the input layer.
notes: Adds one [512] parameter vector (interaction2_scale, zero-init) computed as trigram_emb * fourgram_orig_emb where fourgram_orig_emb is the pure normalized embedding shifted by 3 positions (consistent with all other pure n-gram channels). Also registers it in the scalar AdamW optimizer group. Artifact cost is negligible (~400 bytes after brotli). Motivation: the existing interaction_emb captures (t-2,t-1) token pair signal; this extends the pattern one step earlier to capture (t-3,t-2) pairs, which is useful for multi-token words and common trigram patterns in English.

hypothesis: Denser LAWA sampling (interval 0.0145→0.006, ~2.5× more snapshots during warmdown) plus momentum warmdown coupling (Muon momentum ramps 0.99→0.995 as LR cosine-decays) for more stable low-LR convergence.
notes: Two synergistic changes: (1) more LAWA averaging windows (~97 vs ~40) reduces variance in the final averaged model; (2) warmdown momentum ramp (0.99→0.995 linear over warmdown phase) gives higher effective temporal averaging at low LR, stabilising update direction when steps are small. Both target the same goal: better convergence quality during the 58% warmdown phase.

hypothesis: Reduce label_smoothing from 0.1 to 0.05 to reduce over-regularization: allows the model to target more confident correct predictions during warmdown, better aligning training loss with eval NLL.
notes: Previous z-loss trial revealed softcap makes logits reach ~25 at late training (z^2~625, z_loss~0.0625 ≈ 12% of CE), causing over-regularization. label_smoothing=0.1 is similarly conservative. Reducing to 0.05 halves the smoothing signal so model can be more confident in correct predictions. warmdown_frac=0.72 provides plenty of stable convergence time.

hypothesis: Switch TTT optimizer from SGD+Nesterov to AdamW(betas=(0.9,0.9), wd=0.01) to get per-parameter adaptive LR across heterogeneous layers with fast-converging second moments in the score-first sequential adaptation loop.
notes: AdamW beta2=0.9 means second moments converge in ~6 gradient steps (3 epochs x 2 seqs/GPU/chunk), matching our TTT step budget. Bias correction makes estimates valid from step 1. Weight decay 0.01 is mild (~0.03% cumulative per chunk) preventing drift. AdamW overhead vs SGD is negligible (element-wise ops). The cosine LR schedule still applies via pg['lr'] modification. No NS overhead or eval-budget risk. Literature: PR#807 LoRA-EMA TTT too slow (2434s); 'Test-Time Training Done Right' shows better optimizers help; PR#771 AdamW TTT competitive.

hypothesis: Pull parallel_residual_start from 7→9: layers 7,8 flip parallel→sequential, giving 2 more multi-step refinement layers right after recurrence; layers 9-10 stay parallel.
notes: Residual-topology flip on 2 layers. Zero param / FLOPs delta in our impl (no kernel fusion — sublayers always serialize in Python). Theory: sequential expressivity > parallel per-layer (PRISM arXiv:2602.10796, Universal Transformers); the post-recurrence integration zone (layers 6-8) benefits most from iterative refinement chains. Step-0 forward changes: layers 7,8 mlp now sees post-attn signal instead of x_in. No param-routing or DDP unused-param issues since Block.parallel is set after construction. hypothesis: Lower ema_decay 0.999→0.997 (half-life ~693→~230 steps) to remove ~9% random-init contamination of the post-training EMA checkpoint and confine averaging to the decay phase where the D2Z basin is actually converged.

Lowering to 0.997 cuts init contamination to ~0.06% and gives half-life ~230 steps, which sits inside the late warmdown where LR<0.2*peak and the basin is actually converged — closer to the IMU-1 / D2Z prescription of EMA during the decay phase. Single-axis sweep on a knob that does not appear in the crowded LESSONS.md "AdamW beta/eps/wd/warmdown_frac" cluster.

hypothesis: Skip redundant eval_val_sliding compile+scoring when TTT is enabled (saves ~15-25s eval budget), then use freed budget to restore ttt_epochs from 3→4 for better per-chunk TTT adaptation.

Net: improved TTT adaptation quality with lower eval overrun risk.

hypothesis: Extend WD taper to the scalar optimizer (q_gain, resid_mix, attn_scale, mlp_scale, skip_weights, skip_gates) which previously had constant adam_wd=0.02 throughout training — aligning WD decay with LR decay during warmdown so architectural control params can settle without being pulled toward zero.

During the 72% warmdown phase, LR decays toward 0 while WD stays at 0.02 — a mismatch that shrinks these parameters when the model is supposed to be converging. The fix: tag scalar groups with base_weight_decay=adam_wd and include them in the per-step wd_scale multiplication. WD for scalar params tapers from 0.02 to ~0.003 (15% final_mult) over the warmdown period, freeing them to find sharper final values without WD drag. No architecture change, no new hyperparameters.

PR #1769 confirmed lower sigma (12.85→12) helps; this goes further (to 9.5) while still being far too wide to clip any real weights (9.5σ clipping probability is ~10^-21 for Gaussian).

hypothesis: Replace cosine warmdown (min_lr=0.10) with linear warmdown (min_lr=0.02): per arXiv 2502.15938, linear decay to near-zero outperforms cosine by maintaining higher LR later in warmdown and allowing fuller convergence.

Combined with reducing min_lr from 0.10 to 0.02, this implements the trapezoidal (WSD-linear) schedule that the Straight-to-Zero paper shows beats cosine for LLM pre-training. LAWA weight averaging still starts at warmdown onset (frac=0.42), collecting ~97 snapshots across the decay trajectory.

hypothesis: Long-short SWA: layers 0,1,2,6,7,8 use FA3 causal-local window=512; recurrence (3-5) and parallel-residual (9-10) layers stay full attention — frees ~25-30% of attention compute per forward, translating into more training steps under the 600s budget while preserving global attention where it matters most.

hypothesis: Remove label_smoothing entirely (0.05→0.0) from F.cross_entropy: pure NLL training aligns the training objective with the sliding-window and TTT eval metrics, and the logit softcap + EMA provide sufficient implicit regularisation.

The first trial (exp_174) proved the manual log_softmax+gather approach adds ~67MB extra tensor materialization per step which slows training; this trial isolates whether the label_smoothing=0.0 hypothesis itself helps. TTT uses base_model(x,y) for adaptation — with label_smoothing=0, TTT loss = pure NLL = eval metric, potentially improving TTT convergence.

hypothesis: Replace standard cross-entropy with focal loss (gamma=2) in TTT adaptation step, upweighting high-perplexity tokens to concentrate gradient signal where adaptation is most needed.
notes: Focal loss FL=(1-p_t)^2*CE in the TTT training loop (not scoring). Hard tokens (low p_t, high CE) get weight~1; easy tokens (p_t~1) get weight~0. This focuses TTT adaptation on the high-perplexity portions of each chunk where the quantized model has the most to gain, reducing wasted gradient on already-confident predictions. No change to optimizer, schedule, freeze depth, or chunk size.

hypothesis: Fix fourgram channel to use pure normalized embeddings (consistent with other n-gram channels) + reduce gptq_calibration_batches 64→54 to save ~6s eval time and stay within the 600s eval budget in this environment.

Reducing GPTQ calibration from 64 to 54 batches saves ~6s of Hessian collection time (minimal GPTQ fidelity impact) to clear the eval budget. Combined change: cleaner fourgram signal + reliable eval timing.

hypothesis: Extend batch-curriculum half-batch phase from 20% to 35% of wallclock (aligned to enable_looping_at), covering the entire constant-LR period plus a short early-warmdown slice for more gradient steps before depth recurrence activates.

Mechanistically: max extra gradient steps when LR is highest (0–28%), small-batch fine-grained descent in early warmdown (28–35%), stable large-batch for final convergence under depth recurrence. Fast catch-up effect means late stages don't need long large-batch period.

When TTT runs, 'quantized_ttt' is the primary metric so 'quantized' and 'quantized_sliding_window' are purely diagnostic — skipping them is safe. The del eval_model,compiled_model at line 1575 remains valid since compiled_model refers to the training model (never overwritten when ttt_enabled=True). hypothesis: Set min_lr=0.0 (linear decay-to-zero) instead of 0.02, following arxiv 2502.15938 which shows D2Z consistently outperforms partial-decay schedules by allowing full LR annealing to zero.

Current baseline uses min_lr=0.02 (2% of peak). This changes the decay endpoint from 2% to 0%, letting the model fully converge at the end of warmdown. LAWA averaging across the warmdown phase should still work well. Single-param change, low risk.

hypothesis: Use float64 for GPTQ Hessian Cholesky factorization instead of float32, improving numerical stability for ill-conditioned Hessians (condition number 1e4+) without changing artifact size or format
notes: Float32 has ~7 significant digits; Hessians for LM layers typically have condition numbers 1e3-1e5 due to highly non-uniform token activation frequencies. The double-Cholesky step (cholesky_inverse(cholesky(H))) compounds any ill-conditioning errors. Float64 provides 15 significant digits, handling condition numbers up to 1e11 accurately. The Hinv result is cast back to float32 before the weight quantization loop. No change to artifact format, clip sigmas, damping, or block size — only GPTQ solver precision improves.

hypothesis: Mechanism swap: replace weak mlp_global_drop=0.02 (activation noise in non-looped MLP layers) with embed_noise_std=0.03 (continuous Gaussian perturbation at normalized embedding level, warmdown-coupled via stoch_depth_scale).

Warmdown-coupled (multiplied by stoch_depth_scale) so it decays cleanly to zero over the 72% warmdown. Token-level discrete noise (2%) and stochastic depth unchanged.

hypothesis: Reshuffle layer-wise RoPE schedule from [24,24,24,16,16,16,16,16,8,8,8] to [24,24,24,8,8,8,28,28,8,8,8] — make 3-layer recurrence zone (layers 3-5) NoPE-dominant for iterative content-alignment, concentrate freed RoPE budget on layers 6-7 (post-recurrence re-anchoring with SWA-512). Going NoPE-dominant on the loop (rope_dims=8) frees each pass for full content-only QK, then layers 6-7 (rope_dims=28, with their existing SWA-512 window) sharply re-anchor position before late abstraction. Mean preserved at 176/11=16 so cost-of-RoPE-overhead and parameter count are unchanged.

hypothesis: Replace TTT optimizer from SGD+Nesterov (lr=0.005) to AdamW (lr=0.001, wd=0.0, betas=(0.9,0.999)) to get per-parameter adaptive LR scaling that accumulates cross-chunk importance weighting via the second moment estimate.

SGD+Nesterov with uniform LR is likely suboptimal for few-step TTT adaptation. LR changed from 0.005 to 0.001 (appropriate AdamW scale). Cosine per-epoch LR schedule retained. Focal loss unchanged.

hypothesis: Revert ttt_epochs to 3 (4 was marginally worse) and reduce eval_stride from 64 to 48, using the 55s headroom revealed by the eval-optimization to score 33% more windows with longer per-token context.
